title: Fix f-string formatting bug in error message
description: The error message in validate_input was using string concatenation with a format specifier instead of an f-string, resulting in literal '{field_name}' appearing in logs.
diff:
diff --git a/validation/input_validator.py b/validation/input_validator.py
index 9a0b1c2..d3e4f5a 100644
--- a/validation/input_validator.py
+++ b/validation/input_validator.py
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@ def validate_input(payload: dict[str, Any], schema: dict[str, type]) -> list[str
     for field_name, field_type in schema.items():
         value = payload.get(field_name)
         if value is None:
-            errors.append("Missing required field: {field_name}")
+            errors.append(f"Missing required field: {field_name}")
             continue
         if not isinstance(value, field_type):
             errors.append(f"Field {field_name} expected {field_type.__name__}, got {type(value).__name__}")
comments: quinn: Oops, classic missing f prefix. Approve.
author: rachel
